content:

I've tested on 8 x V100 GPUs in docker and everything works fine. You can use the following params file for testing: https://github.com/eth-act/lighthouse/blob/feat/eip8025/scripts/local_testnet/network_params_eip8025_zkboost_gpu.yaml

author: han0110
content:
New release of `zkboost` is available [`zkboost@v0.4.1`](https://github.com/eth-act/zkboost/releases/tag/v0.4.1), the full change logs can be found [here](https://github.com/eth-act/zkboost/releases/tag/v0.4.0), some highlights:

- Breaking Changes
  - Docker image is now publisded at `ghcr.io/eth-act/zkboost/zkboost` (previously at `ghcr.io/eth-act/zkboost/zkboost-server`)
  - Mock zkVM configuration changes from `mock_proving_time_ms = 5000` to structure like  `mock_proving_time = { kind = "constant", ms = 5000 }`
  - zkVM kind `external` is renamed to `ere` to be more specific
- Added dashboard to view the latest events and proof history if it's enabled in config: `[dashboard] enabled = true`
- Added OTel integration and updated Grafana dashboard to show proof traces

Also opened a PR https://github.com/ethpandaops/ethereum-package/pull/1354 in `ethereum-package` to support the latest release

```
USED_PORTS = {
    constants.HTTP_PORT_ID: shared_utils.new_port_spec(

```

author: barnabasbusa

author: taulepton_
content: So there was one edge case that threw errors and thinking about it a bit more we should probably document it better or maybe add some verification logic. The case was when I wanted to asign 4 gpus to ere server 1 and 4 gpus to ere server 2. If I just used a gpu count then docker would try and assign the same gpus to both servers. When I used specific gpu ids i.e. server 1 gpu: ["1", "2", "3", "4"], server 2: ["5", "6", "7", "8"] then it works fine. Do you think we should handle this case directly in kurtosis validation?

author: barnabasbusa
content: this can be handled at the package level

author: barnabasbusa
content: so ethereum package should do the validation that we don't try to pass the same gpu to 2 services

author: barnabasbusa
content: probably add this as a sanity check in the input parser

author: taulepton_
content: cool I'll add that

author: barnabasbusa
content: so that the config would fail before we even startup the enclave

author: han0110
content:
hmm sorry I was confused, I think it wouldn't prevent multiple zkboost instances because they are deployed as different containers, just tested locally and it still works

In 1353, is that true that multiple zkboost instances will share the same Ere server?

author: taulepton_
content: Yes they use the same ere server

author: taulepton_
content: Does the ere server do any request caching?

author: taulepton_
content: To be honest I don't think we would likely have 2 zkboost instances connected to the same ere server so we might not need to consider that case. What do you think? <@466102668138512394>

author: barnabasbusa
content: so regarding 3018 - maybe this is time to start something like GpuConfig instead of keep overloading service config. <@402080271563751435> what do you think?

author: barnabasbusa
content: serviceConfig is already a massive beast

author: barnabasbusa
content: and this feels very gpu specific

author: barnabasbusa
content: we should probably also not hardcode nvidia as a gpu driver

author: han0110
content: No, Ere doesn't support that yet, that'd probably require hashing the whole input, or require client to provide some global unique identifier to do the dedup (which seems easier).

author: taulepton_
content: I tend to agree. I suspect that shm_size and ulimits may be more generally applicable but given the only current usecase is the gpu deployment maybe its fine to put it in a GpuConfig.

author: taulepton_
content: maybe we don't need to consider it for now?

author: barnabasbusa
content: I'd introduce it in service config also if there is a use case for it

author: barnabasbusa
content: right now I think the only place where it would be used is in gpuconfig
